sections.dtia_summary: This section defines the timeline and conditions for when security interests in personal property become enforceable under Australian law, impacting cross-border data transfers involving security interests in movable assets.

requirements.text: This Act starts to apply to new security agreements at the registration commencement time
This Act starts to apply to security interests arising after commencement
This Act starts to apply to transitional security agreements and interests
This Act starts to apply to new interests in personal property
This Act starts to apply to prescribed personal property
This Act starts to apply to migrated personal property data from Commonwealth, State and Territory registers
Transitional security interests are enforceable against third parties if they would have been so enforceable under law before commencement
Section 73 (priority between security interests and declared statutory interests) applies to declared statutory interests
Section 106 applies to security interests in intellectual property licences made at or after commencement
Chapter 4 (enforcement of security interests) applies only to security interests provided for by security agreements made at or after commencement
A person may only apply to the Registrar for registration of financing statements at or after registration commencement time
The Registrar may only register financing statements at or after registration commencement time
Part 7.2 (Australian laws and those of other jurisdictions) applies only to security interests in collateral that arise at or after commencement
Section 251 applies only to security interests in collateral that arise at or after commencement
Section 252 applies only to security interests in collateral that arise at or after commencement

sections.source_quote: Part 9.3—Initial application of this Act. This Act starts to apply to the following at the registration commencement time (26 months after the Act is given the Royal Assent, or another time determined by the Minister): (a) new security agreements; (b) security interests arising after commencement; (c) transitional security agreements and interests; (d) new interests in personal property; (e) prescribed personal property; (f) migrated personal property data from Commonwealth, State and Territory registers.
sections.relevance_reason: Irrelevant: security interests, not data protection

sections.heading: Section 1 (cont.)
sections.relevance_reason: cross-border data transfer

sections.heading: Section 1 (cont.)
sections.title: Transitional security interest registration requirements
sections.summary: This section establishes rules for registering transitional security interests in personal property. Registrars must specify registration end times if applicable, disclose transitional security agreements, and ensure migrated security interests are perfected before registration commencement. The section defines when registrations are ineffective due to defects and outlines procedures for correcting registration errors.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires specific disclosures for transitional security interests and defines when registrations become ineffective due to defects, impacting cross-border data transfers involving security interests.

requirements.text: State the transitional registration end time as the end time for effective registration if subsection (4) applies
Disclose that personal property is covered by a transitional security agreement
Disclose that the transitional security agreement provides for a migrated security interest
Register financing change statements to remove data if the data is incorrectly registered
Determine a time for removal of data from the register if the data is incorrectly registered
Register financing statements for transitional security interests before registration commencement time
Ensure registration discloses collateral is covered by a transitional security agreement
Registration time for collateral is the registration time
Registration becomes ineffective after the defect correction period unless amended
Registration is ineffective if collateral is not covered by transitional security agreement

sections.source_quote: Section 334(1): If data is registered with respect to personal property in a financing statement under subsection 333(2) on the basis that the property is in a class determined under subsection 333(1), but the personal property is not, in fact, in the determined class, this Act applies as if the data is not, and never has been, included in the register.
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential: security registration rules not directly data compliance

sections.heading: Section 1 (cont.)
sections.title: Circulating asset definitions and control mechanisms
sections.summary: This section defines circulating assets and control mechanisms for security interests. It specifies when personal property is considered a circulating asset, outlines conditions for control over inventory and accounts, and details how security interests are perfected through control. The rules apply to both physical and financial assets.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines what constitutes a circulating asset and control mechanisms for security interests, which impacts how data is transferred and secured across borders.

requirements.text: Personal property is a circulating asset if the secured party has given express or implied authority for transfers free of security interest
Personal property covered by subsection (5) is not a circulating asset if the secured party has control of the property
Personal property is not a circulating asset if it is goods and the security interest is perfected by possession
Secured party has control of inventory if grantor agrees to specific appropriation and compliance with written agreement
Secured party has control of accounts if amounts paid must be deposited into a specified ADI account

sections.source_quote: Section 340(1): For the purposes of this Act, if a grantor grants a security interest in personal property to a secured party, the personal property is a circulating asset if: (a) the personal property is covered by subsection (5) (unless subsection (2) or (3) applies); or (b) in any other case—the secured party has given the grantor express or implied authority for any transfer of the personal property to be made, in the ordinary course of the grantor's business, free of the security interest.
sections.relevance_reason: Security interests and control mechanisms for financial assets

sections.heading: Compilation
